title: perf test a stress test to profile CPU load of podman
body:
perftest uses libpod as golang library and perform various operations on containers.
This can be used as handy tool to stress test and profile CPU usage of libpod. 

Motivation for this tool is have tool, which can generate profiling data, which can be consumed by standard tools like `go tool pprof`. This could he helpful during integration of CRI-O and libpod.

Currently only stress test is `SingleThreadedStressTest`, but can be extended for parallel tests like `MultiThreadedStressTest` and extend operation for pods also.

Signed-off-by: Kunal Kushwaha <kushwaha_kunal_v7@lab.ntt.co.jp>

@kunalkushwaha, could you answer the open question from 
https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1757#pullrequestreview-171450880?

Those were asking whether the proposed tool or a shell script is a better solution to do benchmarking and profiling. You've invested a great deal of time in this PR since then and it would be bad if the common conclusion is to drop this PR and to go for a shell script instead.

@vrothberg : I had updated the PR message as per your suggestion. Sorry didn't updated, you that I had updated.

----
Motivation for this tool is have a tool, which can generate profiling data, which can be consumed by standard tools like `go tool pprof`. This could he helpful during integration of CRI-O and libpod. These things are not possible with shell scripts.

Currently only stress test is `SingleThreadedStressTest`, but can be extended for parallel tests like `MultiThreadedStressTest` and extend operation for pods also. 

I kept it small for initial PR, so its  easy to review and merge.
@kunalkushwaha Are you still working on this?

@rhatdan This PR is complete.
 Additional test as can be added later per requirements.



Some of Tests which have failed are due to ``VarlinkClient`` tests. I same errors are occurring for all pull requests.

```
======================================================================
ERROR: test_999_remove (test.test_pods_ctnrs.TestPodsCtnrs)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/contrib/python/podman/podman/libs/pods.py", line 78, in remove

  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/varlink/client.py", line 84, in _wrapped
    return self._call(method.name, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/varlink/client.py", line 141, in _call
    (message, more) = self._next_varlink_message()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/varlink/client.py", line 111, in _next_varlink_message
    raise e
varlink.error.VarlinkError: {'parameters': {'reason': 'cgroups: cgroup mountpoint does not exist'}, 'error': 'io.podman.ErrorOccurred'}
```
@kunalkushwaha Could you rebase and repush, Seems lots of fixes have been moved into CI, which might require a rebase.

All issues related to the PR are fixed.
-  Updated `vendor.conf` dependencies in `/vendor` i.e. [`github.com/pkg/profile`](github.com/pkg/profile)
-  Since, there is change in signature of function `libpod.WithNetNS`, updated the test for same.

Currently `full_vm_testing` fails due to some integration test related to `rootless tests`, which are not related to this PR.
Cirrus is exploding - seems like only the rootless tests. I think I remember @cevich talking about failing rootless tests in IRC today - was that on master?
Yes, tests are failing on HEAD branch.
/me looking...

...Hmmm other tests failing as well in this PR's case.  I had thought the failures I was seeing were limited to my newer VM images + old runc + needing to passthrough ``$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR``.  Looking at the long list of job failures in Cirrus to see if this is more systemic...

...okay, interesting.  The rootless tests do seem to be failing, including on [master as indicated by the status badge](https://github.com/containers/libpod#continuous-integration-).  I think this will be fixed by #1788, and it seems to be really close to passing tests.
This LGTM... Let's see about getting this merged. Might need another rebase to fix the tests?
looking
ya, need rebase for PASS.  It's failing on the 'rootless' tests we fixed (last?) week.
Need to fix this PR with changes regarding https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1904/files#diff-76939214d3033492b329833fb18b7088R574. Will look into tomorrow.

I don't have a direct answer to your question, but wonder if the unittest failure indicates anything related:

```
# github.com/containers/libpod/contrib/perftest
contrib/perftest/main.go:102:4: cannot use !true (type bool) as type "github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/image/types".OptionalBool in field value
contrib/perftest/main.go:106:45: too many arguments in call to client.ImageRuntime().New
	have (context.Context, string, string, string, *os.File, *image.DockerRegistryOptions, image.SigningOptions, bool, bool)
	want (context.Context, string, string, string, io.Writer, *image.DockerRegistryOptions, image.SigningOptions, bool)
```

I see that in both the Fedora and Ubuntu results.

@kunalkushwaha It looks like that's a side effect of the image volumes work from @umohnani8 - it seems like there's a volume somewhere in your create config that has a relative path, which I don't believe is supported since the image volumes work landed.

I don't see any volumes named "roc", though... @umohnani8 Could this be an issue with image volumes interacting with named volumes? Only place I can think an unexpected volume would pop up from
@mheon I feel this __roc__ volume is /proc , which is somehow getting  truncated.

That would make sense...

Alright, I think I know what needs to happen here, but it could end up being a lot of code. We need to actually record the named volumes in the container config, as opposed to just adding them to the spec, which will allow us to more accurately detect whether we have named volumes in the container.

@mheon I got the issue. and currently testing the fix with all test cases. Will make PR. 
thanks for pointing to namedVolume.
@kunalkushwaha Ack - glad to hear!

_ctr.runtime.config.VolumePath_ is empty, due to which, condition satisfy and _proc_ is manipulated.
check with len(ctr.runtime.config.VolumePath) > 0 && .. should fix it.

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/boltdb_state_internal.go#L569

Ugg, I think that's the mount/cgroups error from the python tests, yes?  That's a known flake, though frustrating, just smash the 're-test' button if you see it.

It's a "This Should Never Happen" kind of things.  We spent days trying to reproduce / figure that problem out. Somehow/why it happens in GCE more than on our laptops  :confounded:
